plan_metadata.name: Sample Hello World Meeting Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "Search samples" input box in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Type 'my first meeting app' into the search/filter input box above the samples list in the "Samples" tab of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button for "My First Meeting App" in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:hello-world-in-meeting}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Deploy" button under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code to execute the deploy lifecycle stage.
Click the "Deploy" button in the confirmation dialog that asks "Do you want to deploy resources in dev environment?" within Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there's "Ok to proceed? (y)" exist in terminal log.
Click the "y" key prompt in the terminal of Visual Studio Code to confirm proceeding with the installation of @azure/static-web-apps-cli.
Press Enter key in the VS Code terminal to confirm the prompt "Ok to proceed? (y/n)" during Azure Static Web Apps deployment using the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
@assertion there's notification: "actions in deploy stage executed successfully." exist
Click the "Home" tab in the Microsoft Teams web app's meeting interface to switch the main view to the Home section.
Click the "Remove" option in the Home tab context menu within the Microsoft Teams web interface, highlighted by a red crosshair in the tab drop-down menu.
Click the "Remove" button in the confirmation dialog that asks "Want to remove this tab?" in the Microsoft Teams web application, to permanently remove the Home tab from the "testing" chat interface.
